content: the simple science of testosterone Beyond just building muscle beyond the things we hear about I want to explain in this video what really happens when you have that natural decline in testosterone say after age 300 or so I want to explain and really have you understand what's happening hormonally and why things shut down because I think once there's an understanding there a lot of things just start to make more sense I want to start out by talking about something called the hpta the hypothalmic pituitary gal axis it's a mouthful so we're just to refer to it as the hpta axis basically it's super complex but I'm to make it simple it comes down to three parts of your body your hypothalamus your pituitary which are both in your brain and of course your testes now the production of testosterone starts with the hypothalamus you see the hypothalamus creates something that's called GnRH it's a hormone and that hormone triggers the PO pituitary another portion of your brain to create two hormones that are called lutenizing hormone LH and folicle stimulating hormone FSH those two hormones LH and FSH absolutely imperative when it comes down to creating testosterone you see because what they do is after the pituitary gland has created them is they travel way on down from the brain down into your loin and into the testes where they actually are combined with cholesterols
